texoclaim-chain memory
agentledgerdrift
stream

claim-chain memory · append-only · receipts on every answer

Memory that knows
when to stop believing things.

Every fact you state becomes a journaled claim. When a fact changes, the old claim isn't buried in a vector index — it'sstruck, sealed with a receipt, and superseded on the record. Git tracks code diffs. texo tracks claim diffs.

journal · canonicalfrontier —open the ledger ↓
the supersession

A fact changed. Here's the receipt.

Teach a fact. Contradict it later. The old claim doesn't linger — it's retired on the record, with what replaced it, when, and from which source line.

Alice owns release approval.

meeting_notes.md:3 · seq 09

retired byreceipt 42abd965 · seq 15

Ben owns release approval now.

meeting_notes.md:5 · current

The heuristic floor can't separate these — same subject, different words. TheQwen relation judge retires the superseded claim and surfaces genuine contradictions as conflicts instead of silently trusting the newer sentence.

the live ledger

Current truth, stale record, open conflicts.

Replayed from the journal at this frontier. Backend offline — showing the seeded ledger.

current0
    stale0
      conflict0
        the party trick

        texo audits its own docs.

        Feed the repo's own prose back through the pipeline and its stale architecture claims come back superseded — with receipts — by the docs that replaced them. The thesis, demonstrated on the tool.

        see the drift →
        texo · claim-chain memoryevery answer carries its receipts
